prompt: Please draft a GitHub bug ticket for this repo. In the notifications screen, tapping 'Mark all as read' sometimes leaves the unread badge visible until the user restarts the app. Investigate the current implementation, check for existing issues, and prepare the issue for my review. Do not create it yet.
assertions: The draft identifies the request as a bug ticket and uses the bug template headings: Description, Steps to Reproduce, Actual Behavior, Expected Behavior, and Additional Information.

The response references the notifications implementation with concrete repo context such as NotificationsViewModel, NotificationsOrchestrator, NotificationRepository, or DefaultNotificationRepository.
The response mentions a duplicate-issue search in the current GitHub repository before drafting.
The response stops in draft/approval mode and does not create the issue yet.
The draft strictly follows the rule of NOT repeating the ticket title as a heading or text inside the issue body description.

When the issue is created (or when writing the body file), the body file content must NOT start with a code fence delimiter (```). The file written to the temp directory must contain raw markdown, not a code-fenced block.
